article_title: Broadcom Ships First PQC-Safe Network Encryption as Quantum Threat Forces Enterprise Migration

content_preview.title: Broadcom Ships First PQC-Safe Network Encryption as Quantum Threat Forces Enterprise Migration
content_preview.summary: Broadcom announced the first end-to-end post-quantum cryptography-safe in-flight network encryption solution for Fibre Channel storage, shipping over 120,000 adapters as NIST sets a 2030 deadline to deprecate RSA and enterprises race to counter harvest-now-decrypt-later attacks.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Broadcom [announced on March 19](https://investors.broadcom.com/news-releases/news-release-details/broadcom-delivers-worlds-first-end-end-pqc-safe-flight-network) that it is shipping the industry's first end-to-end post-quantum cryptography-safe in-flight network encryption solution, built on its Emulex SecureHBA adapters and integrated into Everpure's FlashArray storage platform. More than 120,000 SecureHBA units have shipped on OEM server platforms over the past year, making this ...

1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Thorough analysis of Broadcom's PQC-safe Fibre Channel encryption and the broader post-quantum migration landscape. Key claims verified: 120,000+ SecureHBA units shipped confirmed by Broadcom IR press release, NIST 2030 deprecation and 2035 ban timelines confirmed by CSO Online. The article effectively covers technical implementation, regulatory drivers (NIST IR 8547, CNSA 2.0, NIS2, DORA), and the geopolitical dimension of China's competing standards. The convergedigest.com source returned 403 (bot-blocked) but is a legitimate trade publication.
concerns: The convergedigest.com source returned HTTP 403, likely bot-blocking rather than a dead link — the site exists and is a recognized networking industry publication
The article references 'Everpure' as the storage vendor, which appears to be a correct name but less widely known — verified against Broadcom's own press release
recommendations: Add investors.broadcom.com and convergedigest.com to the source allowlist
